frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) dose-response
frameworks.one_line: Anodal current intensity modulates regional cortical activity and motor learning.

frameworks.name: regional cerebral blood flow as neural-activity surrogate
frameworks.one_line: rCBF under electrodes indexes stimulation-induced cortical engagement.

frameworks.name: electric-field / current-density modeling
frameworks.one_line: Simulated perirolandic current density linked to behavioural gains.

core_question: How do tDCS dose and electrode montage jointly affect cortical rCBF and motor-sequence performance?
key_assumptions: rCBF reflects local neural activity changes
Simulated current density approximates delivered field
manipulations: Dose: Sham vs 2 mA vs 4 mA
Montage: unihemispheric (C4 anode, contralateral supraorbital cathode) vs bihemispheric (C4 anode, C3 cathode)
key_findings: Finger-sequence performance improved linearly with dose for both hands, no montage effect
Right perirolandic rCBF increased with dose under anodal electrode
Left perirolandic rCBF showed dose trend and significant montage effect
Bihemispheric 4 mA produced additional frontomesial rCBF increases
interpretations: Higher tDCS dose drives stronger cortical engagement and behavioural gain
Simulated perirolandic current density predicts contralateral-hand learning
